investigation: Wave 27: Construction Monopoly x Finanzas-Politicas Unified Cross-Reference
methodology: Systematic cross-referencing of construction-cartel.json, odebrecht-lava-jato-argentina.json, monopolios-construccion.json, kirchnerismo-financial-map.json, capital-flight-wave2.json, monopolios-wave25-offshore-deep.json, monopolios-concesiones.json, monopolios-crossref-obras-publicas.json, and monopolios-energia.json to identify overlapping actors, shared jurisdictions, campaign finance flows, and infrastructure monopoly connections.

1_actors_in_both_investigations.title: Actors Appearing in BOTH Finanzas-Politicas AND Monopoly Investigations
1_actors_in_both_investigations.description: Individuals and entities that appear across the political finance investigation (campaign donations, patrimony declarations, judicial cases) and the construction/monopoly investigation (cartel membership, public works contracts, market concentration). These dual-presence actors represent the nexus between political power and economic monopoly.

political_figures_linked_to_construction.name: Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner
political_figures_linked_to_construction.finanzas_politicas_role: Principal defendant in Causa Vialidad (6 years, confirmed by Supreme Court). Principal defendant in Cuadernos (203 counts of passive bribery). House arrest.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.construction_connection: Directed assignment of 51 road works (ARS 46B) to Lazaro Baez's Austral Construcciones in Santa Cruz. Cuadernos trial alleges she led the illicit association that collected 10-20% kickbacks from all major construction companies.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.overlap_strength: DIRECT — convicted of fraudulent administration of construction contracts

political_figures_linked_to_construction.name: Julio De Vido
political_figures_linked_to_construction.finanzas_politicas_role: Convicted in Causa Once (4 years), Causa GNL (4 years). Prosecuted for illicit enrichment (USD 690K unjustified). Planning Minister 2003-2015.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.construction_connection: His ministry controlled ALL public works contracting during Kirchner era. Named in Odebrecht case (AySA plants, gas pipelines). Oversaw the entire Club de la Obra Publica system. Ranked top contractors: Techint > Electroingenieria > IECSA.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.overlap_strength: DIRECT — central node connecting political finance to construction cartel distribution

political_figures_linked_to_construction.name: Roberto Baratta
political_figures_linked_to_construction.finanzas_politicas_role: Convicted in GNL case (3.5 years). On trial in Cuadernos (main bribe collector). Identified as receiving USD 1.5M+ in bribes.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.construction_connection: Secretary of Energy under De Vido. Named in Odebrecht AySA and gas pipeline cases. Calcaterra admitted payments to Baratta for 2013/2015 campaigns. Driver Oscar Centeno documented his cash collections from construction companies.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.overlap_strength: DIRECT — primary intermediary between construction companies and Kirchner political apparatus

political_figures_linked_to_construction.name: Jose Lopez
political_figures_linked_to_construction.finanzas_politicas_role: Unified sentence: 13 years prison. Caught at monastery with USD 9M cash. Secretary of Public Works.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.construction_connection: Named in Odebrecht AySA and Sarmiento cases. As Secretary of Public Works, directly authorized construction contracts. Prosecutors linked the USD 9M to Odebrecht bribe payments (not formally proven).
political_figures_linked_to_construction.overlap_strength: DIRECT — authorized construction contracts and personally received cash

political_figures_linked_to_construction.name: Ricardo Jaime
political_figures_linked_to_construction.finanzas_politicas_role: Convicted: 8 years (enrichment), 6 years (Once tragedy). Imprisoned at Ezeiza since Nov 2024.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.construction_connection: Transport Secretary. Roggio admitted paying 5% of transport subsidies to Jaime. Called to indagatoria in Odebrecht Sarmiento case. Assets found: USD 4M airplane, yacht, apartments.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.overlap_strength: DIRECT — received kickbacks from Roggio (construction/transport) and implicated in Odebrecht

political_figures_linked_to_construction.name: Lazaro Baez
political_figures_linked_to_construction.finanzas_politicas_role: Convicted: 15 years unified sentence (money laundering USD 54.87M + Vialidad fraud). Fine: USD 329M.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.construction_connection: Owner of Austral Construcciones. Monopolized Santa Cruz road construction: 51 contracts, 80% of provincial road works. ARS 46B in contracts. Alleged Kirchner testaferro.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.overlap_strength: DIRECT — construction monopolist AND political finance launderer

political_figures_linked_to_construction.name: Mauricio Macri
political_figures_linked_to_construction.finanzas_politicas_role: President 2015-2019. Family patriarch Franco Macri founded SOCMA holding.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.construction_connection: SOCMA/IECSA: 17% of first road concessions (1988). 3 instances in 1982 cartel complaint. Cousin Calcaterra ran IECSA during Kirchner era. Calcaterra formed consortium with Odebrecht. Macri's AFI chief Arribas received USD 594K from Odebrecht. Macri government paid USD 759M for stalled Sarmiento project. Campaign received Braskem (Odebrecht subsidiary) donation of ARS 500K.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.overlap_strength: DIRECT — family construction empire, Odebrecht connections on both sides (cousin + intelligence chief)

political_figures_linked_to_construction.name: Alberto Fernandez
political_figures_linked_to_construction.finanzas_politicas_role: Prosecuted in Causa Seguros (insurance monopoly benefiting broker Martinez Sosa). Gender violence charges pending.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.construction_connection: Indirect. Wife Malena Galmarini chaired AySA (the same entity at center of Odebrecht bribery). Galmarini denounced for irregular USD 127M chlorine contract with Filiberti.
political_figures_linked_to_construction.overlap_strength: INDIRECT — via AySA connection (wife's role at Odebrecht-implicated entity)

construction_companies_linked_to_politic.name: Techint / Rocca family
construction_companies_linked_to_politic.construction_role: Top state contractor across all governments since 1947. Steel duopoly (Ternium). Tube monopoly (Tenaris/SIAT). USD 22B revenue. Gasoducto NK: USD 500M pipe supply (sole bidder).
political_finance_links: Cuadernos: executives implicated (obtained controversial dismissal). 4 instances in 1982 cartel complaint.
Lava Jato: Confab (Techint subsidiary) paid USD 10.4M to Petrobras executive. Paolo Rocca prosecuted in Italy (acquitted on jurisdiction).
Transfer pricing via Uruguayan shells: 15-22% of gross profits shifted offshore. Shell equity: USD 5.66B.
Milei era: Sacde+Techint consortium winning new gas infrastructure. Also suing state for USD 96M + USD 640M.

construction_companies_linked_to_politic.name: Grupo Roggio
construction_companies_linked_to_politic.construction_role: 22% of first road concessions (1988). Buenos Aires subway since 1994. ARS 8B in state contracts 2003-2012. Odebrecht consortium partner for AySA plant.
political_finance_links: Cuadernos arrepentido: admitted paying 5% of transport subsidies to Ricardo Jaime.
11 bribery payments (Jun 2009-Sep 2010) totaling ARS 5.9M documented.
Received 22 works from Vialidad Nacional worth ARS 3.3B+.
Odebrecht: processed for AySA plant bribery (oral trial).
Restitution offered: USD 1,267,108.
offshore_jurisdictions: BVI (Gotland International, Graymark International, Linhill International — all incorporated 2016)

construction_companies_linked_to_politic.name: IECSA / SACDE (Macri -> Calcaterra -> Mindlin)
construction_companies_linked_to_politic.construction_role: 17% of road concessions under SOCMA (1988). 3rd largest state contractor under Kirchner. Odebrecht consortium partner (30%) for Sarmiento burial. Now owned by Mindlin, renamed SACDE.
political_finance_links: Calcaterra: arrepentido in Cuadernos — admitted payments to Baratta for 2013/2015 Kirchner campaigns.
Contradicted himself: denied bribes in Odebrecht case before different judge.
Casacion removed him from Cuadernos trial (June 2024, challenged).
Odebrecht: USD 20M in estimated bribes for Sarmiento project via fictitious Spanish contract.
Family connection: Macri cousin. 'Sold' to avoid conflict when Macri became president.
offshore_jurisdictions: Delaware (shared entity between Calcaterra and Mindlin per reporting)
construction_companies_linked_to_politic.mindlin_vertical_integration: Mindlin now controls SACDE (construction) + pending acquisition of Loma Negra (45.6% cement market) + Pampa Energia (energy) + Edenor (electricity distribution) — unprecedented vertical integration risk

construction_companies_linked_to_politic.name: Electroingenieria / Ferreyra
construction_companies_linked_to_politic.construction_role: 2nd largest state contractor under Kirchner (energy infrastructure). Pilar power plant: USD 300M -> USD 565M (88% cost overrun, sole bidder). USD 1B in Cordoba public works.
political_finance_links: Cuadernos: Ferreyra imprisoned, refused to cooperate. Supreme Court ruled against him Nov 2024.
Swiss documents complicated bribery case (2021).
Close relationship to Kirchner governments + Cordoba governor De la Sota.
construction_companies_linked_to_politic.family_offshore: Isabel Ferreyra Aycinena linked to BVI entities: Zalver Investments Limited (2005), Rosestone Investments Limited (2013)

construction_companies_linked_to_politic.name: Cartellone (Jose Cartellone Construcciones Civiles)
construction_companies_linked_to_politic.construction_role: Cartel member. Built Vega creek entubement with Roggio and Supercemento (USD 177M). Odebrecht consortium partner for AySA Parana de las Palmas plant.
political_finance_links: Cuadernos: executives implicated.
Odebrecht: processed as consortium partner for AySA plant (16.66% share).
Named as local partner through which Odebrecht made bribe payments.

construction_companies_linked_to_politic.name: Austral Construcciones / Lazaro Baez
construction_companies_linked_to_politic.construction_role: Near-total monopoly of Santa Cruz road construction. 51 contracts, ARS 46B. Founded 2003 (same year as Kirchner presidency). 50% of works incomplete. Bankrupt 2017.
political_finance_links: Causa Vialidad: Supreme Court confirmed conviction. Cristina Kirchner convicted for directing contracts to Baez.
Ruta del Dinero K: EUR 55M laundered through Swiss banks in 6 months via Elaskar/Farina.
Money laundering conviction: USD 54.87M. Fine: USD 329M.
Alleged Kirchner testaferro (frontman).
offshore_jurisdictions: Uruguay (El Entrevero farm purchase — convicted Dec 2025)
Belize, Panama, Seychelles (Elaskar entities, disputed direct K connection)

1_actors_in_both_investigations.note: Every major construction cartel member appears in BOTH the political finance investigation AND the monopoly investigation, confirming the construction-politics feedback loop is structurally inherent, not incidental.

2_shared_offshore_jurisdictions.title: Shared Offshore Jurisdictions Between Political Actors and Construction Monopolists
2_shared_offshore_jurisdictions.description: Mapping of offshore jurisdictions used by both political figures (from finanzas-politicas) and construction monopolists (from monopoly investigation), revealing a shared infrastructure of financial opacity.

construction_monopolists: Roggio family: Gotland International (2016), Graymark International (2016), Linhill International (2016), + BVI trust via Trident Trust (2004). Lucia Roggio also on Gotland and Linhill.
Madanes family: DQ Assets Limited (Javier Madanes Quintanilla, 2014), The Hastings Trust (Carmen + Judith Madanes, 2000, defunct).
Blaquier family: Yamary Business Ltd (Carlos Herminio Blaquier, 2014).
Vila family: LSAR Holding Ltd (Jose Vila Recolons, 2000).
Ferreyra family: Zalver Investments (2005), Rosestone Investments (2013) — via Isabel Ferreyra Aycinena.
political_actors: Daniel Munoz (Nestor Kirchner's secretary): linked to BVI entities in offshore leaks.
Gustavo Arribas (Macri AFI chief): received USD 594K from Hong Kong shell (RFY Import & Export Ltd) controlled by Odebrecht.
jurisdiction_matrix.overlap_note: BVI is the most common jurisdiction across both investigation streams. Construction families created entities there primarily in 2014-2016, coinciding with the final Kirchner presidency and Macri transition.

construction_monopolists: Techint/Rocca: Tenaris and Ternium listed in Luxembourg. Primary holding jurisdiction.
ArcelorMittal Acindar: parent company in Luxembourg.
Eurnekian/Corporacion America: Corporacion America Airports S.A. holding in Luxembourg.
jurisdiction_matrix.overlap_note: Luxembourg serves as the corporate holding jurisdiction of choice for the steel duopoly and infrastructure concessionaires. No direct political figure links, but the companies themselves are major state contractors and concessionaires.

construction_monopolists: Techint/Rocca: multiple shell companies with no productive activity. USD 5.66B equity. 15-22% profit shifting.
Lazaro Baez: El Entrevero farm purchase (convicted Dec 2025 for money laundering).
political_actors: Capital flight wave2: USD 3.5B in Argentine deposits in Uruguay.
Multiple blanqueo declarations originating from Uruguay.
jurisdiction_matrix.overlap_note: Uruguay is the nearest offshore haven for both construction profits (Techint transfer pricing) and political money laundering (Baez). The country's banking secrecy historically enabled both.

construction_monopolists: Roggio: bank accounts opened via BVI companies (Pandora Papers).
Electroingenieria/Ferreyra: Swiss documents complicated bribery case (2021).
Austral/Baez: EUR 55M laundered through Swiss banks in 6 months via Elaskar/Farina.
political_actors: Gustavo Arribas: Credit Suisse account in Zurich received Odebrecht transfers.
UBS/Credit Suisse: DOJ settlement USD 511M (2025) for helping clients hide $4B+ offshore.
Blanqueo 2024: Switzerland among top 4 countries of origin for declared assets.
jurisdiction_matrix.overlap_note: Switzerland is the preferred destination for illicit proceeds from BOTH construction kickbacks (Ferreyra, Baez) AND political bribery (Arribas/Odebrecht). The Credit Suisse/UBS settlement confirms systemic facilitation.

political_actors: Federico Elaskar: alleged 40-45 offshore companies in Belize, Panama, Seychelles (linked to Kirchner money laundering network, disputed).
jurisdiction_matrix.overlap_note: Panama serves both food/retail monopolists (Coto, Blaquier/Ledesma) and the Kirchner-linked money laundering network.

political_actors: Odebrecht Sarmiento case: USD 4.5M of fictitious 'technical assistance' payments routed through Andorra for bribery.
jurisdiction_matrix.overlap_note: Andorra appears specifically in the Odebrecht bribe routing for the Sarmiento burial project — the construction project that connected Odebrecht, IECSA/Calcaterra, Ghella, and Comsa.

construction_monopolists: Eurnekian: multi-layered structure — Liechtenstein foundation controls BVI holding which controls Luxembourg and Spain sub-holdings.
jurisdiction_matrix.overlap_note: Eurnekian's Liechtenstein foundation sits atop his airport/infrastructure empire (AA2000, 35 airports, 90% of Argentine air traffic).

construction_monopolists: IECSA/SACDE: evidence suggests Calcaterra retained hidden control through Delaware offshore entity shared with Mindlin.
political_actors: Capital flight wave2: Delaware LLCs used extensively for anonymous real estate and brokerage in US. Formation cost: $50. No state-level beneficial ownership disclosure.
jurisdiction_matrix.overlap_note: Delaware's opacity enables anonymous ownership of both construction companies (IECSA) and capital flight vehicles.

3_cartel_companies_in_top_contractors.title: Construction Cartel Companies Appearing in Top Contractors List (Neo4j Graph)
3_cartel_companies_in_top_contractors.description: Cross-referencing the Club de la Obra Publica members against the top contractors found in the Neo4j graph database (PublicContract nodes). The graph primarily captures procurement/licensing contracts rather than public works, which explains the dominance of telecom companies.

Telecom_combined.note: Telecom Argentina STET (243) + Telecom Personal (93) + Telecom Argentina SA (71) + Cablevision (13, Telecom subsidiary). Controlled by Magnetto/Clarin group, then sold to Werthein.

YPF_combined.note: YPF SA (179+66+12) + YPF Gas (47+27). State-controlled oil company.

Ledesma.note: Ledesma SAAI (117+8+additional). Blaquier family. Sugar/paper monopolist.

Telefonica_combined.note: Telefonica Moviles (217+64) + Telefonica de Argentina (37+additional). Spanish telecom.

AMX_Claro.note: AMX Argentina (222+65). Carlos Slim. Mexican telecom.

cartel_companies_found_in_graph.note: Roggio appears but with minimal procurement contracts — confirms the graph data does NOT capture public works construction contracts, where Roggio held 22% of road concessions and received ARS 3.3B+ from Vialidad Nacional.

3_cartel_companies_in_top_contractors.gap_analysis: The Neo4j graph captures licensing/procurement contracts (telecom, pharma, food) but is missing the entire public works construction layer. The top contractors by government contract count (Telecom 411, YPF 284, Ledesma 129) represent a different facet of state-business relations than the construction cartel. However, the controlling families overlap:

family_overlap_between_contract_types.in_graph: Telecom Argentina — 411 contracts (largest contractor in graph)
family_overlap_between_contract_types.in_construction: Not a direct construction company, but Magnetto's Clarin Group controls media narrative around construction corruption. Telecom infrastructure contracts involve physical construction.
Not a construction company, but Blaquier family has BVI offshore (Yamary Business Ltd, Cabonor International). Represents agroindustrial monopoly intersecting with same offshore infrastructure.

family_overlap_between_contract_types.in_graph: Not directly found as contractor (operates via subsidiaries: Ternium, Tenaris, SIAT, Tecpetrol)
family_overlap_between_contract_types.in_construction: Top construction contractor across all administrations. Steel duopoly. USD 500M sole-bid pipe contract.
22% road concessions. ARS 3.3B+ in Vialidad works. Subway monopoly. Odebrecht partner.
Controls SACDE (ex-IECSA). Pending Loma Negra acquisition (45.6% cement). Energy monopoly via Pampa/Edenor.

3_cartel_companies_in_top_contractors.recommended_ingestion: To close this gap, a future data wave should ingest Vialidad Nacional/CONTRATAR portal data and the 71 companies named in the Cuadernos case as PublicWorksContract nodes.

4_odebrecht_monopoly_infrastructure_conn.title: Odebrecht Connections to Monopoly Infrastructure Actors
4_odebrecht_monopoly_infrastructure_conn.description: How Odebrecht's Argentine operations intersected with domestic monopoly actors in airports, toll roads, energy, and water infrastructure.

connections.monopoly_sector: Airports (35 airports, 90% of commercial air traffic). Energy (CGC, TGN gas transport).
connections.odebrecht_connection: INDIRECT. Eurnekian's Corporacion America acquired IECSA post-Calcaterra (per monopolios-argentina-sectores.json). IECSA was Odebrecht's primary Argentine consortium partner (30% of Sarmiento burial). Eurnekian also controls CGC and co-controls TGN (40% of gas transport) — the same gas pipeline infrastructure where Odebrecht paid USD 25M in bribes.
connections.infrastructure_overlap: Gas pipelines: Odebrecht bribed officials for gas pipeline contracts (GNEA, TGN/TGS expansion). Eurnekian controls CGC (gas production) and co-controls TGN (gas transport). The pipeline infrastructure is physically connected.
connections.connection_strength: MODERATE — shared infrastructure sector and corporate lineage (IECSA)

connections.monopoly_sector: Buenos Aires subway (all 6 lines since 1994). Rail (Ferrovias Urquiza). Water (Aguas Cordobesas).
connections.odebrecht_connection: DIRECT. Roggio was Odebrecht's consortium partner for AySA Parana de las Palmas plant (16.67% share). Aldo Roggio processed and elevated to oral trial in Odebrecht-AySA case. Also named as local partner through which Odebrecht made bribe payments.
connections.infrastructure_overlap: Transport + water. Roggio operates transport concessions and was Odebrecht's partner in water infrastructure (AySA). Same official (Ricardo Jaime) received kickbacks from BOTH Roggio's transport subsidies AND was implicated in Odebrecht's Sarmiento project.

connections.monopoly_sector: Construction, energy (Pampa Energia, wind parks), cement (pending Loma Negra).
connections.odebrecht_connection: DIRECT. IECSA held 30% of the Sarmiento burial consortium with Odebrecht. Calcaterra admitted bribe payments in Cuadernos (to Baratta). Odebrecht routed USD 4.5M through Andorra for Sarmiento bribes via fictitious Spanish 'technical assistance' contract.
connections.infrastructure_overlap: Construction + energy. SACDE (successor to IECSA) now wins gas pipeline contracts alongside Techint under Milei. Mindlin's Pampa Energia generates the energy that flows through the pipeline infrastructure Odebrecht bribed to build.

connections.monopoly_sector: Steel (Ternium/Acindar duopoly). Pipes (Tenaris/SIAT). Construction (Techint Ingenieria). Oil & gas (Tecpetrol).
connections.odebrecht_connection: INDIRECT in Argentina. DIRECT in Brazil: Techint subsidiary Confab paid USD 10.4M to Petrobras executive (Lava Jato). Paolo Rocca prosecuted in Italy for Petrobras bribes (acquitted on jurisdiction). In Argentina, Techint and Odebrecht operated in the same gas pipeline infrastructure sector.
connections.infrastructure_overlap: Gas pipelines: Techint/SIAT supplied pipes (USD 500M sole bid for Gasoducto NK). Odebrecht built the pipeline network (GNEA). The pipes + construction represent vertical integration of the same infrastructure. Both companies paid kickbacks to the same officials (De Vido, Baratta).
connections.connection_strength: MODERATE in Argentina (same sector, same officials). DIRECT in Brazil (Lava Jato).

connections.odebrecht_connection: DIRECT. Cartellone was Odebrecht's consortium partner for AySA Parana de las Palmas plant (16.66% share). Named as 'local partner' through which Odebrecht made bribe payments to officials.

connections.monopoly_actor: Toll road concessionaires (AUSOL/Abertis, Autopistas del Sol)
connections.monopoly_sector: Highway concessions (Buenos Aires area, national routes).
connections.odebrecht_connection: INDIRECT. Odebrecht's gas pipeline projects ran alongside highway corridors. The same officials (De Vido, Planning Ministry) controlled both highway concessions and pipeline contracts. The PPP highway round (2018, USD 12.5B) featured many of the same cartel companies that worked with Odebrecht.
connections.connection_strength: WEAK — shared regulatory apparatus but no direct partnership

connections.monopoly_sector: Cement (45.6% market share via Loma Negra).
connections.odebrecht_connection: INDIRECT but significant. Camargo Correa is a SEPARATE Brazilian construction giant also implicated in Lava Jato. Won the AySA Berazategui plant contract (separate from Odebrecht's AySA Parana plant). Both Brazilian companies operated the same bribery playbook in Argentina. Camargo Correa's InterCement now in judicial restructuring — pending sale to Mindlin.
connections.connection_strength: MODERATE — parallel Brazilian corruption scheme in same sector

4_odebrecht_monopoly_infrastructure_conn.key_finding: Odebrecht's Argentine operations were entirely embedded within the existing construction cartel structure. They did not create a new corruption network — they plugged into the Club de la Obra Publica through local partners (Roggio, Cartellone, IECSA, Supercemento) who already had the political relationships and kickback mechanisms in place.

5_campaign_finance_from_construction_mon.title: Campaign Finance Flows from Construction Monopolists to Political Parties
5_campaign_finance_from_construction_mon.description: Documented campaign finance connections between construction companies/monopoly actors and political parties, including both legal donations and illegal 'black money' flows.

documented_flows.mechanism: Fundraising dinner at Sociedad Rural, March 18, 2015 (ARS 50,000/plate)
documented_flows.status: Legal, disclosed. But Braskem later pled guilty alongside Odebrecht in US.

documented_flows.mechanism: Direct payments to Roberto Baratta. Admitted as arrepentido in Cuadernos.
documented_flows.status: Illegal — companies with public works contracts prohibited from donating.

documented_flows.amount: 10-20% of contract values (per Wagner confession). Total road works alone: ARS 3.3B+ in Roggio's case.
documented_flows.mechanism: Cash collected by Ernesto Clarens (financial operator), converted to USD, delivered to officials. Documented in Centeno notebooks. Portion used for electoral campaign financing.
documented_flows.status: Illegal — entire mechanism exposed in Cuadernos case. 86 defendants on trial.

documented_flows.amount: 5% of transport subsidies (ARS 5.9M documented in 11 payments, Jun 2009-Sep 2010)
documented_flows.mechanism: Direct cash returns from subsidy payments. Roggio confessed as arrepentido.
Campaign contribution. Criba simultaneously held Buenos Aires contracts exceeding USD 180M.

documented_flows.destination: Alleged financing of Cristina Kirchner and Amado Boudou campaigns
documented_flows.mechanism: Diversion from Madres de Plaza de Mayo housing program. Schoklender alleged campaign financing.
documented_flows.status: Criminal case — extinguished by Bonafini's death (2022).

5_campaign_finance_from_construction_mon.structural_finding: Argentine campaign finance law (since 2019) technically allows corporate donations but prohibits donations from companies holding public works contracts. In practice, per Poder Ciudadano (Transparency International chapter), most campaign money remains 'black' (undeclared). The construction cartel's 10-20% kickback mechanism served as the primary off-books campaign financing vehicle for multiple administrations.
5_campaign_finance_from_construction_mon.bipartisan_nature: Both Kirchnerism (via Cuadernos mechanism) AND Cambiemos/PRO (via Odebrecht/Braskem donation, Criba contribution) received construction monopolist money. The same companies funded opposite political blocs.

6_cuadernos_monopoly_overlap.title: Cuadernos Case Defendants Who Are Monopoly Actors
6_cuadernos_monopoly_overlap.description: Cross-reference of the 86 Cuadernos defendants with actors identified in the monopoly investigation, identifying which defendants control monopoly positions in Argentine markets.

monopoly_defendants.name: Paolo Rocca (Techint)
monopoly_defendants.cuadernos_status: Executives implicated. Obtained sobreseimiento (dismissal) — widely criticized, appeal filed.
monopoly_defendants.monopoly_position: Steel duopoly (Ternium: flat steel). Tube near-monopoly (Tenaris/SIAT). Richest Argentine (USD 7.3B). Top state contractor across ALL administrations since 1947.
monopoly_defendants.market_power: EXTREME — controls steel inputs for all construction + pipe supply for all gas infrastructure

monopoly_defendants.name: Aldo Roggio (Grupo Roggio)
monopoly_defendants.cuadernos_status: Arrepentido. Confessed 5% kickbacks on transport subsidies. Offered USD 1,267,108 restitution.
monopoly_defendants.monopoly_position: Buenos Aires subway monopoly (all 6 lines + Premetro since 1994). Cordoba water concession. Rail concession. 22% of road concessions (1988).
monopoly_defendants.market_power: HIGH — natural monopoly via transport and water concessions

monopoly_defendants.name: Angelo Calcaterra (IECSA/SACDE)
monopoly_defendants.cuadernos_status: Arrepentido. Admitted payments to Baratta. Casacion removed from trial (June 2024, challenged).
monopoly_defendants.monopoly_position: At time of offenses: 3rd largest state contractor. Consortium partner with Odebrecht. Now: company sold to Mindlin (SACDE), who may acquire Loma Negra (cement monopoly).

monopoly_defendants.name: Gerardo Ferreyra (Electroingenieria)
monopoly_defendants.cuadernos_status: Imprisoned, refused cooperation. Supreme Court ruled against him (Nov 2024).
monopoly_defendants.monopoly_position: Dominant energy infrastructure contractor under Kirchner. Sole-bidder contracts (Pilar plant). USD 1B in Cordoba public works over 17 years. Nuclear sector advocate.
monopoly_defendants.market_power: HIGH — energy infrastructure concentration in Kirchner era

monopoly_defendants.name: Carlos Enrique Wagner (Esuco / Chamber of Construction)
monopoly_defendants.cuadernos_status: Arrepentido. Confessed entire cartel mechanism as ex-president of Argentine Chamber of Construction.
monopoly_defendants.monopoly_position: Head of the cartel coordination body (La Camarita). Also named in Odebrecht gas pipeline case as intermediary who told Odebrecht that money was needed for officials.
monopoly_defendants.market_power: MODERATE individually, but CRITICAL as cartel coordinator

monopoly_defendants.name: Executives of Cartellone, Supercemento, Decavial, Losi, Panedile, Coarco, Equimac
monopoly_defendants.cuadernos_status: Various — some arrepentidos, some indicted, some processed.
monopoly_defendants.monopoly_position: All cartel members. Operated within the Club de la Obra Publica rotating bid assignment system.
monopoly_defendants.market_power: MODERATE individually, SIGNIFICANT collectively as cartel

non_construction_monopolists_in_cuaderno.note: The Cuadernos case focused specifically on the construction sector. 71 companies, primarily construction firms. However, the financial operators (Ernesto Clarens) and political figures (De Vido, Baratta, Lopez) also appear in the monopoly investigation through their roles in assigning energy, transport, and infrastructure concessions to monopoly actors.
non_construction_monopolists_in_cuaderno.clarens_role: Ernesto Clarens served as the financial intermediary converting construction kickbacks to USD. His role parallels the Uruguayan shell companies used by Techint for transfer pricing — different mechanism, same function (extracting value from the system).

6_cuadernos_monopoly_overlap.key_finding: Of the top 5 construction companies in Argentina, 4 are represented among Cuadernos defendants (Techint, Roggio, IECSA/Calcaterra, Electroingenieria). The 5th (Austral/Baez) is convicted separately in Causa Vialidad. This means 100% of major Argentine construction monopolists have been implicated in systematic bribery.

7_monopoly_family_crossref.title: Monopoly Family Names Cross-Referenced Across All Investigation Files
7_monopoly_family_crossref.description: Systematic search for 18 monopoly family names across all finanzas-politicas and monopoly research files.

families.name: Rocca (Techint)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in BOTH investigations. Construction cartel founding member (since 1947). Cuadernos implicated. Lava Jato convicted (Brazil). Offshore: Luxembourg + Uruguay. USD 7.3B net worth.

families.name: Roggio
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in BOTH. Cuadernos arrepentido. Odebrecht co-defendant. Subway monopolist. Offshore: BVI (3 companies, 2016) + Switzerland + USA. Pandora Papers.

families.name: Mindlin
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation. Acquired IECSA (renamed SACDE). Pending Loma Negra acquisition. Controls Pampa Energia + Edenor. Vertical integration risk: construction + cement + energy.

families.name: Macri
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in BOTH. SOCMA/IECSA: cartel member since 1977. 3 instances in 1981 complaint. Cousin Calcaterra: Odebrecht partner + Cuadernos arrepentido. AFI chief Arribas: Odebrecht payment recipient.

families.name: Ferreyra (Electroingenieria)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in BOTH. Cuadernos defendant (imprisoned, refused cooperation). Energy infrastructure monopolist. Isabel Ferreyra Aycinena: BVI offshore (Zalver Investments 2005, Rosestone Investments 2013).

families.name: Eurnekian (Corporacion America)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation. Airport monopoly (AA2000, 35 airports, 90% traffic). IECSA acquirer. Gas transport (TGN). Offshore: Liechtenstein + BVI + Panama + Luxembourg. Pandora Papers. Concession extended to 2038 without competitive rebidding.

families.name: Magnetto (Clarin/Telecom)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation. Telecom Argentina: 411 contracts (LARGEST contractor in Neo4j graph). Media monopoly (Clarin + Telefonon + Ole + TN). Not directly in construction cartel.

families.name: Bulgheroni (Pan American Energy / Axion)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation (energy sector). Not directly in construction cartel or Cuadernos.

families.name: Coto
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation (retail). Offshore: Leopold Company S.A. (Panama, 2012). Not in construction cartel.

families.name: Braun (La Anonima)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation (Patagonian retail). Not directly in construction cartel or Cuadernos.

families.name: Perez Companc
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in BOTH investigations. Named in 1981 cartel complaint (multiple instances). Historical construction cartel member. Sold SOCMA-linked holdings. Now primarily in food/agro via Molinos.

families.name: Pagani (Arcor)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation (food sector). Not in construction cartel.

families.name: Blaquier (Ledesma)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Ledesma has 129 contracts in Neo4j graph. Offshore: Alejandro Blaquier — Cabonor International (Panama, 2006). Carlos Herminio Blaquier — Yamary Business Ltd (BVI, 2014). Not in construction cartel but shares offshore infrastructure.

families.name: Cartellone
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in BOTH. Cuadernos implicated. Odebrecht consortium partner (AySA plant, 16.66%). Cartel member.

families.name: Werthein
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation (telecom, acquired Telecom Argentina from Clarin). Offshore: Dario Werthein — Canrold Overseas Limited. Not in construction cartel.

families.name: Vila / Manzano
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation (media). Vila-Manzano group (Grupo America, Telefe acquisition). Jose Vila Recolons: LSAR Holding Ltd (BVI, 2000). Manzano: ex-Interior Minister under Menem, transitioned to media business.

families.name: Madanes (ALUAR)
families.cross_investigation_finding: Present in monopoly investigation (aluminum monopoly). Offshore: Javier Madanes Quintanilla — DQ Assets Limited (BVI, 2014). Carmen + Judith Madanes — The Hastings Trust (Cook Islands, 2000, defunct). Not in construction cartel but aluminum is construction input.

8_capital_flight_construction_nexus.title: Capital Flight Channels Used by Construction Monopolists
8_capital_flight_construction_nexus.description: How construction monopoly profits flow into the capital flight channels documented in capital-flight-wave2.json.

users: Techint/Rocca (USD 5.66B in shell equity, 15-22% profit shifting)
channels_used.link_to_capital_flight: Techint's Uruguayan shells are the single largest documented transfer pricing structure in Argentina. USD 3.5B total Argentine deposits in Uruguay.

users: Arribas (Credit Suisse Zurich for Odebrecht bribes)
channels_used.link_to_capital_flight: Switzerland among top 4 blanqueo origin countries. UBS/Credit Suisse settled USD 511M with DOJ for facilitating offshore hiding.
BVI/Panama entities enable anonymous ownership of assets worldwide. Used by construction and non-construction monopolists alike.
Luxembourg serves as the 'respectable' offshore — listed corporate structures that enable profit routing.

users: IECSA/Calcaterra-Mindlin (shared Delaware entity for hidden ownership)
channels_used.link_to_capital_flight: Delaware requires no beneficial ownership disclosure. $50 formation cost. Enables anonymous real estate and brokerage.

8_capital_flight_construction_nexus.aggregate_estimate: Construction monopolists documented here have at minimum USD 5.66B in offshore shell equity (Techint Uruguay alone), plus unknown amounts in BVI/Panama/Liechtenstein structures, plus EUR 55M documented laundering (Baez). The total is certainly in the tens of billions of dollars — a significant fraction of the USD 483B in Argentine external assets documented in the capital flight investigation.

synthesis.core_finding: The construction cartel is not merely a corruption case — it is the structural mechanism through which Argentine monopoly capital and political power reinforce each other. Every major construction monopolist has been implicated in bribery (Cuadernos, Odebrecht, or both). The same families that dominate steel (Rocca), construction (Roggio, Macri/Calcaterra/Mindlin, Ferreyra), and cement (Camargo Correa -> Mindlin) also maintain offshore structures in the same jurisdictions used for political money laundering. Campaign finance flows bidirectionally: construction monopolists funded both Kirchnerism AND Cambiemos/PRO.
structural_patterns: PATTERN 1 — The same 5-6 families have dominated Argentine construction since the military dictatorship (1976). The 1981 cartel complaint names the same families as the 2025 Cuadernos trial: Rocca, Macri, Roggio, Perez Companc.

PATTERN 2 — Every change of government produced a change in FAVORED contractor, not a change in the SYSTEM. Kirchner: Electroingenieria + Austral. Macri: IECSA/SACDE. Milei: Techint + SACDE. The cartel persists.

PATTERN 3 — Offshore structures serve dual purposes: (a) corporate profit shifting (Techint Uruguay, Eurnekian Liechtenstein) and (b) personal enrichment/bribery proceeds (Roggio BVI, Ferreyra family BVI, Baez Switzerland). The same jurisdictions (BVI, Switzerland, Luxembourg, Uruguay) appear in both channels.

PATTERN 4 — Vertical integration is accelerating. Mindlin's potential control of SACDE (construction) + Loma Negra (cement) + Pampa Energia (energy) + Edenor (electricity distribution) would create a self-dealing loop unprecedented even by Argentine standards.

PATTERN 5 — The Odebrecht case proves international corruption networks plugged directly into the domestic cartel. Odebrecht did not corrupt a clean system — they paid through existing local partners (Roggio, Cartellone, IECSA, Supercemento) who already had the bribery infrastructure.

PATTERN 6 — Zero Odebrecht convictions in Argentina as of March 2026, despite USD 35M in confessed bribes, confirmed by US court guilty plea. The judicial system has been structurally unable to prosecute international construction corruption.

quantitative_summary.total_overpricing_documented_usd: 300,000,000+ (Odebrecht gas pipelines alone) + 387% overpricing on Austral/Baez Santa Cruz works

metadata.data_quality: HIGH for cartel structure, judicial proceedings, offshore entities (sourced from fiscal accusations, ICIJ leaks, court records, Odebrecht plea deal). MEDIUM for campaign finance flows (many are off-books by design). LOW for current offshore balances (no disclosure requirements).
